project_type: React + TypeScript board game adaptation with Express 5 WebSocket server
build_system: Vite for client, tsx for server
verification_instructions: 1. Run TypeScript compilation to verify no type errors. 2. Verify the logic manually by reading code.
environment_constraints: Open internet available. No automated tests exist - verify via TypeScript compilation.
contribution_requirements: Work directly on main branch. CSS in App.css uses minified single-line blocks.
key_patterns: Sequential per-player pattern (from harvest): 1) GameState has a currentPlayerId field, 2) Popup component checks localPlayerId vs currentPlayerId - shows waiting if not your turn, 3) Client sends WS action in online mode, 4) Server validates playerId matches currentPlayerId before processing, 5) After processing, state advances to next player's ID and broadcasts. The kami resolution popup uses kamiResolutionActive + kamiResolutionStep='showing' to determine visibility. KamiResolutionTemple has winnerId field for each sanctuary.
directory_structure: src/components/ (React components), src/store/ (Zustand store with gameStore.ts), src/utils/ (gameLogic.ts with game logic functions), src/server/ (Express+WS server), src/types/ (TypeScript types), src/i18n/ (translations)
